description: Close browser-context test coverage gaps in the Plugin SDK identified during a line-by-line audit. All stories add missing tests for real browser edge cases — QuotaExceededError on storage writes, MutationObserver custom options, async predicate rejection, symbol serialization without description, and combined abort+timeout signal interaction in fetchFromPage.

userStories.title: Add QuotaExceededError and custom options tests for storage and DOM utilities
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want tests verifying that setLocalStorage/setSessionStorage silently handle QuotaExceededError and that observeDOM respects custom ObserveDOMOptions, so that these browser-context edge cases have regression coverage.
acceptanceCriteria: storage.test.ts has a test for setLocalStorage that mocks localStorage.setItem to throw a DOMException with name 'QuotaExceededError' and verifies no error is thrown
storage.test.ts has a test for setSessionStorage that mocks sessionStorage.setItem to throw a DOMException with name 'QuotaExceededError' and verifies no error is thrown
dom.test.ts has a test for observeDOM with { attributes: true, childList: false } that verifies the callback fires on attribute changes but NOT on child additions
userStories.notes:
Files to edit:
- platform/plugin-sdk/src/storage.test.ts (lines 68-88 for setLocalStorage, 155-175 for setSessionStorage)
- platform/plugin-sdk/src/dom.test.ts (lines 190-249 for observeDOM section)

For QuotaExceededError tests, follow the same pattern as the existing SecurityError tests but mock the setItem method to throw: `Object.defineProperty(globalThis, 'localStorage', { value: { setItem: () => { throw new DOMException('Storage quota exceeded', 'QuotaExceededError'); } }, configurable: true, writable: true });`

For observeDOM custom options, create a container element, call observeDOM with { attributes: true, childList: false }, then: (1) change an attribute on the container and verify callback fires, (2) add a child element and verify callback does NOT fire. The test environment uses happy-dom's MutationObserver.

userStories.title: Add async rejection, symbol serialization, and combined signal tests for timing, log, and fetch utilities
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want tests verifying that waitUntil handles async predicate rejections, log serializes Symbol() without description correctly, and fetchFromPage correctly distinguishes timeout from user abort when both signals are present, so that these edge cases have regression coverage.
acceptanceCriteria: timing.test.ts has a test 'keeps polling when async predicate rejects' that passes an async function returning Promise.reject() and verifies waitUntil keeps polling until the predicate eventually resolves to true
log.test.ts has a test that logs Symbol() (no argument) and verifies the serialized output is '[Symbol: ]' (empty description, not '[Symbol: undefined]')

fetch.test.ts has a test that creates an AbortController, passes both its signal and a short timeout to fetchFromPage hitting the /slow endpoint, and verifies the resulting ToolError has code 'TIMEOUT' and category 'timeout' (proving the timeout signal is correctly distinguished from the user signal)
userStories.notes:
Files to edit:
- platform/plugin-sdk/src/timing.test.ts — add test near line 295 (after the 'keeps polling when predicate throws' test). Pattern: `await waitUntil(async () => { count++; if (count < 3) return Promise.reject(new Error('async fail')); return true; }, { interval: 20, timeout: 2000 });`
- platform/plugin-sdk/src/log.test.ts — add test in the 'safe serialization' describe block (near line 170). `log.info('test', Symbol()); expect(entries[0]?.data).toEqual(['[Symbol: ]']);`
- platform/plugin-sdk/src/fetch.test.ts — add test in the 'fetchFromPage' describe block (near line 337). Create an AbortController (do NOT abort it), pass both signal and timeout:100 to fetchFromPage hitting /slow, assert the ToolError has code 'TIMEOUT'. The test HTTP server's /slow endpoint responds after 5000ms, so the 100ms timeout will fire first.
